title: EPR Countries requirements and most important questions
bodyHtml:
The purpose of this article is to guide you in understanding the basic information related to EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) through the FAQ section, as well as to present the requirements for each country and how to meet your compliance obligations.

 

This does not constitute legal advice. You are encouraged to check your legal obligations and your compliance status with your legal advisor.  For more assistance, you can reach out to our partner Taxually.

 

I- Basics / Glossary:

  • EPR: Extended Producer Responsibility. This refers to a set of laws requiring sellers who market certain products to contribute to recycling and waste management under the polluter-pays principle.
  • WEEE or DEEE: Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment or “Déchets d'Équipements Électriques et Électroniques” in French.
  • Eco fee: this is the fee amount due by the producer to the PRO for the products placed on the market. This is a statutory fee set by the PRO.  
  • Eco-ID or EPR number: This is the registration number of a producer with a PRO
  • PRO: Product Responsibility Organisation : it is the environmental agency in charge of a specific scheme (for example, WEEE, Packaging and so on) in a specific country.

II- FAQ:

What is Back Market's role regarding sellers ?

When facilitating the sale of products in certain countries, marketplaces may have a legal obligation to collect sellers' eco-IDs and verify them. If the seller does not provide their valid eco-IDs, , Back Market is required:

  • for products placed on the French market, to  pay eco-fees on behalf of sellers;
  • for products placed on the German market: to suspend your account from selling in Germany the categories of products for which you do not hold a valid eco-ID
  • for products placed on the Spanish market (Packaging): if your business is established outside of Spain and you do not provide a valid Spanish Packaging EPR registration number, Back Market may be legally required to report your packaging volumes to a PRO and to remit EPR fees on your behalf, then charge you accordingly (see “How does billing work for EPR?” below).

 

In which countries will Back Market require your EPR numbers ? 

France, Germany, Spain and Austria have imposed EPR obligations on marketplaces. Other countries may be added in the future.

For the moment, you can input your EPR numbers in your seller back office for France and for Germany. We will keep you informed as soon as we are in the capacity to collect data for other countries. Where the feature is available, you will be able to enter your EPR numbers directly in your Back Office.

 

In which categories will Back Market require your EPR numbers ?

You may require different EPR numbers depending on the categories of products that you place on the market. Back Market collects your EPR numbers for the product categories concerned in France and in Germany:

  • Packaging
  • Batteries
  • WEEE

You are only required to be registered for the categories of products that you place on the market. For example, if you do not place on the market any products containing batteries, you are not required to obtain an EPR number for the Batteries category.

 

If you place products on the Spanish market, you are required to obtain an EPR number for the Packaging category. Back Market will require your number and may verify it if you are not based in Spain. We will keep you informed when the back office is ready to collect this data (or when new requirements apply).

 

I have entered the EPR same number several times; is that acceptable/correct?

No, this is not correct. A different EPR number is required for each category (WEEE, Packaging, Batteries) and for each country. . This means you can upload up to six different EPR numbers :

 

  1. FR EPR Packaging number
  2. FR EPR Batteries number
  3. FR EPR WEEE number
  4. DE EPR Packaging number
  5. DE EPR Batteries number
  6. DE EPR WEEE number

If additional countries/categories are enabled in the Back Office (for example Spain Packaging), you will be required to provide a separate valid number for those as well.

 

What is the consequence if a  seller fails to provide their EPR numbers?

  • For France:
    Back Market will report your sales to the PROs on your behalf and will pay the eco-fees to the PROs. The seller will be charged by Back Market for these  fees for each order concerned and for each non-compliant category. See the question "How does billing work for EPR?" below.

 

  • For Germany:
    The Packaging category is mandatory for Germany. If the seller does not provide an EPR number for Packaging, they will be taken offline and will no longer be able to sell in Germany.
    You will also be required to share your EPR numbers for the WEEE and Batteries categories in order to be able to continue selling in Germany.

 

  • For Spain (Packaging):
    If your business is established outside of Spain and you do not provide a valid Spanish Packaging EPR registration number, Back Market is legally required to declare the packaging volumes you placed on the Spanish market to a PRO and to remit the corresponding EPR fees on your behalf. Back Market will then charge the EPR fees to your account, along with a reporting fee (where applicable).
    If your business is established in Spain, Back Market will not declare or pay on your behalf. You remain responsible for reporting your packaging volumes and paying EPR fees directly to your PRO and for complying with Spanish requirements.

How much are the EPR fees in France?

Please consult the official price lists of each PRO for detailed information (Article for EPR in France & Article for EPR in Germany). Depending on your specific situation and on the volume of products your place on the marketplace, you may be eligible to either the normal scheme or the simplified scheme.

 

As an example, the EPR fee applicable to smartphones sold from January 1st 2025 are :

 

  • For the WEEE category:
    • €4.42 for the simplified scheme
    • €2.56 for the normal scheme (€2.46 if the battery is removable by the user)
  • For the Packaging category: 0,1554 € for the simplified scheme. For the normal scheme : please refer to the PROs.
  • For the Batteries category: 0,30 € per battery for the simplified scheme. For the normal scheme : please refer to the PROs.

Where and how can sellers obtain their EPR numbers?

Sellers can obtain their EPR numbers from authorized PROs in their respective countries. In some situations, sellers may need to appoint a representative in order to register with a PROs.

For France:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
    • https://www.ecosystem.eco
    • https://www.ecologic-france.com
  • Batteries
    • https://www.corepile.fr/
    • https://www.batribox.fr/
  • Packaging
    • https://www.citeo.com
    • https://www.leko-organisme.fr
    • Or reach out to our partner Taxually

 

For Germany:

 

  • Waste from Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE)
    • https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
  • Batteries
    • https://www.stiftung-ear.de/en/home
  • Packaging
    • https://lucid.verpackungsregister.org/
    • Or reach out to our partner Taxually

For Spain:

 

  • Packaging
    • https://www.ecoembes.com/en
    • https://procircular.es/
  • Sellers not established in Spain must also appoint an Authorized Representative to register and comply.
  • Or reach out to our partner Taxually

Where and how can sellers enter their EPR numbers?

Sellers can enter their EPR numbers in the Back Office under the "My Profile" section. You can then click on "Your business profile" and then "Legal details and documents." After that, scroll down to find the "Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)" section, where you can submit your EPR numbers for both France and Germany, for the 3 required categories.

Where the Spain feature is available, you will be able to add your Spanish Packaging EPR registration number under the same EPR section by selecting Spain.

 

What is the format of an EPR number ?

  • In France: The correct format of FR EPR numbers should be the following:
    • FR EPR Packaging number: FRXXXXXX_XXXXXX
    • FR EPR Batteries number: FRXXXXXX_XXXXXX
    • FR EPR WEEE number: FRXXXXXX_XXXXXX

 

  • In Germany: The correct format of DE EPR numbers should be the following:
    • DE EPR Packaging number: DEXXXXXXXX (8 characters after “DE”)
    • DE EPR Batteries number: DEXXXXXXXXXXXXX (13 characters after “DE”)
    • DE EPR WEEE number: DEXXXXXXXX (8 characters after “DE”)

 

  • In Spain: The format of the Packaging EPR registration number may vary depending on the registration route/PRO and your setup (for example, via an Authorized Representative). Back Market may verify the validity of the number against official sources.

Back Market may verify the accuracy of the EPR numbers entered using the official databases.

 

Are second-hand products exempt?

Second hand products may be exempt provided they were already put on the local market and have therefore already been subject to EPR fees. For example, a smartphone that was already put in the market in France and was subject to France WEEE is exempt from WEEE when it is resold in France. Please note that imported products may not benefit from the exemption. For example, if you sell a second-hand smartphone in Germany that was sourced in France, the smartphone will still need to be subject to German WEEE. 

 

Exemptions do not apply to the Packaging category. Exemptions for second hand products can only apply to the WEEE and Batteries categories.

 

  • Sellers claiming exemption must indicate this in the dedicated "I am exempt" section in the Back Office under the WEEE/DEEE category.
  • If you already have communicated a WEEE/DEEE number in the format “FRXXXXXX_XXXXXXX”, no further action is needed.
  • We are currently working on implementing the same "I am exempt" feature in the Back Office, allowing you to inform us of an exemption for the Batteries category.

Does the seller need to provide specific documents at one stage of the process?

  • Exemptions: there is no document to upload, you only to tick the box "I am exempt" on the Back Office
  • For France: For France EPR numbers, there are no documents to upload.
  • For Germany: and for EPR Packaging only, we will ask you to upload a certificate of registration on top of the EPR number itself. This document should be uploaded in the Back Office directly.
  • For Spain: For Packaging, we require your Spanish Packaging EPR registration number (where applicable) and may verify it against official sources. No additional document upload is required unless communicated otherwise.

What resources can I use to get more information or check my compliance status?

  • SSC article: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) : obligations in France
  • SSC article: Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR): Obligations in Germany
  • Check your Back Office directly (“My profile” > “Your business profile” > “Legal details and documents” > “"Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)"), and check if you have communicated EPR numbers and if their format is correct.
  • We encourage you to contact our partner Taxually for assistance with EPR regulations, covering all the countries where Back Market operates.

How does billing work for EPR?

First of all, we encourage you to review our T&Cs, where all details related to EPR billing are outlined.

 

For France:

Back Market will report your sales and pay the EPR fees on your behalf, if you have not provided your valid EPR numbers for WEEE, Batteries and Packaging.  Back Market will charge these EPR fees reported on your behalf per category and on a quarterly basis. (e.g., billing for sales made in Q1, from January to March, will occur in April).

 

As a seller, if you become compliant in a specific category during the year, you are responsible for reporting the entire year directly to the PROs. Back Market does not handle this reporting; therefore, we provide retroactive refunds for EPR fees already billed for that year.

 

These retroactive refunds are also processed on a quarterly basis, occurring in the quarter following the submission of your EPR number and compliance in a given category. The line that will appear on your invoice will be as follows: “Regularization_EPR_France_Year_Quarter_WEEE/Packaging or Batteries”

 

In addition, Back Market will also charge a service fee called “EPR reporting fee” of € 50 per quarter and per category, for each category in which you have not provided your valid EPR number. These service fees are not refundable in case you become compliant during the year.

 

For Germany:

Back Market will not charge any eco-fees or any reporting fees to your account. Indeed, you are required to be compliant and to pay eco-fees yourself to the relevant PRO. If you are not compliant, Back Market is required to suspend your account in Germany.

 

For Spain:
 
If your business is established outside of Spain and you do not provide a valid Spanish Packaging EPR registration number, Back Market is legally required to declare packaging volumes placed on the Spanish market to a PRO, pay the corresponding EPR fees on your behalf, and charge these fees to your account (and a reporting fee where applicable). You will be charged based on the weight and composition of the packaging you put on the Spanish market.


 
If you provide a valid EPR number and it is validated, Back Market will stop declaring and invoicing packaging EPR fees for Spain. You remain responsible for declaring your packaging volumes and paying your EPR fees directly to your PRO.


 
If your business is established in Spain, Back Market will not declare or pay on your behalf. You remain responsible for reporting and payment directly to your PRO and for complying with Spanish requirements (including any applicable registry obligations).

 

III-Countries checklist requirements:

France EPR checklist:

  • Mandatory categories: WEEE, Packaging, and Batteries
  • Collection method: Sellers have to register, otherwise we report on your behalf and we take the money from your payout
  • Consequences if not compliant: you may incur fees (see the question "How much are the fees in France?")

Germany EPR checklist:

  • Mandatory categories: Packaging only for the moment
  • What to expect for the future: Enforcement of Batteries and WEEE most likely, as we did for Packaging
  • Collection method: No marketplace collection, but sellers are obliged to register themselves
  • Consequences if not compliant: If you do not provide us with an EPR number for the Packaging category, you will not be allowed to sell in Germany, and the market will be set to "offline" in your Back Office.

Spain EPR checklist:

  • Mandatory categories: Packaging (mandatory EPR obligations for packaging apply starting January 1, 2025)
  • What to expect for the future: Enforcement of Batteries and WEEE may follow
  • Collection method:
    • If your business is established in Spain: No marketplace collection; sellers are obliged to register and report themselves (including registering with the public producer registry, where applicable).
    • If your business is established outside of Spain: you must provide a valid Spanish Packaging EPR registration number; if you do not, Back Market may be legally required to declare/pay on your behalf and charge you accordingly.
    • Consequences if not compliant (outside Spain): Back Market will charge you the packaging EPR fees (and a reporting fee where applicable) based on packaging volumes placed on the Spanish market.
